# Module 4 Checkpoint

Module 4 does not introduce new project steps. This is your **mid-project checkpoint** — a structured opportunity to confirm that Steps 1, 2, and 3 are complete and your product is ready for the final build phase in Module 5.

:::{.callout-important}
Step 4 (Grounded Generation and Evaluation) begins in Module 5. Do not start Step 4 until your vector index from Step 3 is validated and saved to Google Drive.
:::

# Progress Checklist

Use this checklist to confirm your team's status before Module 5.

## Step 1 — Industry Scoping and Filing Acquisition

- [ ] NAICS 4-digit industry code documented and justified
- [ ] 5-10 companies selected and confirmed as 10-K filers
- [ ] 10-K HTML filings downloaded for 2015-2025
- [ ] `filing_metadata.csv` exported with all required SEC fields
- [ ] All 10-K Items extracted and saved with standardized filenames

## Step 2 — Item Cleaning and EDA

- [ ] Domain-relevant Items selected and tied to analytical questions
- [ ] Cleaning pipeline implemented and documented
- [ ] `cleaned_item_metadata.csv` exported
- [ ] EDA section complete with token distributions and coverage tables

## Step 3 — Chunking, Embeddings, and Vector Index

- [ ] Token-aware chunking implemented with chunk-level metadata
- [ ] `naics_code` included in every chunk metadata row
- [ ] Embeddings generated and saved to Drive
- [ ] FAISS vector index built and persisted
- [ ] At least 5 test retrieval queries run and reviewed

# What to Do This Week

1. Review all three notebooks and clean up any incomplete cells
2. Confirm all required output files exist in your Drive at `project_sec10k_rag/data/outputs/`
3. Run at least 3 additional retrieval test queries and note any failure patterns
4. Write one paragraph per team member summarizing their contribution so far

## Checkpoint Evidence to Bring Forward

For the checkpoint, teams should be prepared to show concrete notebook evidence, not only verbal status updates. Bring forward:

- one corpus summary table from Step 2 or Step 3
- one example of chunk-level metadata
- one embedding or vector-index summary
- one retrieval sanity-check table with top-k results
- one short list of known failure patterns or open design decisions

If your retrieval is weak, say so clearly. Honest diagnosis at this stage is more valuable than moving to generation with a brittle foundation.

:::{.callout-note}
If your team is behind on Steps 1-3, use Module 4 entirely for catch-up. Do not advance to Step 4 with an incomplete retrieval foundation — generation quality depends entirely on retrieval quality.
:::

:::{.callout-tip}
Teams can borrow a useful practice from strong prior SEC RAG projects: each notebook section should briefly explain `what was done`, `why it was done`, and `what artifact was saved`. This makes the final four-notebook package much easier to grade and reuse.
:::

# Weekly Team Standup

Module 4 is a checkpoint week, making the standup especially important. The instructor uses this week's standup to assess whether teams are ready to proceed to Step 4.

> **Reference:** [Atlassian — How to Run Effective Standups](https://www.atlassian.com/agile/scrum/standups)

Three questions for the team:

1. *What did the team complete since the last standup?*
2. *What will the team work on before the next standup?*
3. *What blockers or decisions is the team stuck on?*

## In-Person Sections

Standup happens live at the start of class. The Module 4 standup should include a brief status confirmation for each Step 1-3 checklist item above.

## Online Sections — Weekly Standup Document

Submit a **one-page standup document** to the course portal by the due date. Ungraded on its own, but **standup submissions count toward your final project participation grade**.

Use this template:

---

**Team Name / NAICS Industry:**

**Standup Week:** Module 4 — Checkpoint

**Date Submitted:**

| Question | Team Response |
|---|---|
| What did we complete this week? | |
| What will we work on next week? | |
| What is blocking us or needs a decision? | |

**Checkpoint status summary** (one sentence per step):

- Step 1 status:
- Step 2 status:
- Step 3 status:

**Team member who facilitated this standup:**

---

:::{.callout-important}
The Module 4 standup document should be your most detailed one. Instructors use it to decide whether to approve a team's readiness for Step 4 or recommend targeted catch-up work.
:::
